title: westringia saxatilis
intro: Westringia saxatilis, also known as the coastal rosemary, is a shrub-like plant native to Australia, recognized by its grey-green foliage and small blue flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, colds, and flu due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory compounds. Its health benefits include reducing inflammation, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. Coastal rosemary is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal properties.
